text: The hallway, bathed in diffuse artificial light and encased in glass, feels sterile and anticipatory—as though I stand at the threshold of potentiality. Yet, as I pass into the colorful, text-filled exhibit, a sense of curiosity stirs within me. I am surrounded by messages of research and development, words swirling with innovation, and subtle reminders of the limits of perception. I feel disoriented, like the slanted camera, aware of how easily my own vision and understanding can become blurred. The environment provokes reflection on the fragility of clarity and the playful boundary between confusion and discovery.
